title: Antifragile
subtitle: Things That Gain from Disorder
author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb
excerpt: Some things benefit from shocks — they thrive and grow stronger when exposed to volatility, randomness, and stress. Nassim Taleb introduces a concept that had no name: antifragility. The opposite of fragile is not robust; it is antifragile. The book explores how antifragility operates in biology, finance, technology, and everyday life — and why the modern obsession with eliminating volatility has made us dangerously fragile.
